title: Direction-of-Arrival Estimation (narrowband array processing)
sub_domain: Spatial spectral estimation with sensor arrays
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Schmidt 1986 MUSIC; Roy-Kailath 1989 ESPRIT; Krim-Viberg 1996 array processing review; Stoica-Nehorai 1989 CRLB for DoA.

E.description: K narrowband plane-wave sources impinge on an L-element uniform linear (or arbitrary) sensor array; the array-manifold matrix A(theta) in C^{L x K} encodes the steering-vector per angle. Each snapshot is y[t] = A(theta) s[t] + n[t] with source vector s in C^K and sensor noise n ~ CN(0, sigma^2 I).
E.forward_model: y[t] = A(theta) * s[t] + n[t];  A_lk(theta) = exp(-j*2*pi*d_l*sin(theta_k)/lambda)
E.world_state_x: Set of DoA angles {theta_1, ..., theta_K} in (-pi/2, pi/2)
E.observation_y: Array snapshots Y in C^{L x T}, T = number of snapshots

W.regime: Identifiable when K <= L-1 for ULA; CRLB proportional to 1/(T * SNR * array_aperture); coherent sources require spatial smoothing; sub-wavelength (super-resolution) regimes rely on MUSIC/ESPRIT subspace projection or L1-min.

C.solver_class: beamforming (conventional, Capon/MVDR), MUSIC, Root-MUSIC, ESPRIT, SPICE, L1-SVD, sparse Bayesian, ML grid-search
C.complexity: MUSIC: O(L^3 + L^2*T + L*Ngrid); ESPRIT closed-form; L1-min O(Ngrid^3) or O(K^2 * L * Ngrid) with OMP
